reason: The remediation modifies the GRUB2 bootloader configuration to add a superuser password. This is a bootloader change, which the policy classifies as too dangerous to remediate automatically due to the risk of rendering the system unbootable or locking out console access if the password is misconfigured.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potential bootloader lockout; Loss of console/physical access if password is forgotten; System may fail to boot after misconfiguration
